expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 1: ["base"] section header is present
profiles.toml line 2: repository = "/mnt/nas/backup" under the ["base"] section
profiles.toml line 3: password-file = "/root/.restic-password" under the ["base"] section

expectations.text: base 프로필에 keep-daily=7, keep-weekly=4, keep-monthly=12, after-backup=true가 포함됨
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml lines 6-9: [base.retention] section contains keep-daily = 7, keep-weekly = 4, keep-monthly = 12, after-backup = true — all four required values present
profiles.toml line 13: inherit = "base" under the ["documents"] section

expectations.text: documents 프로필 backup 섹션에 /srv/documents가 source로 포함됨

expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 18: inherit = "base" under the ["photos"] section

expectations.text: photos 프로필 backup 섹션에 /srv/photos가 source로 포함됨

expectations.evidence: The TOML file uses valid quoted table headers (["base"], ["documents"], ["photos"]), subtables ([base.retention], [documents.backup], [photos.backup]), and standard key = value pairs throughout. Structure is syntactically valid TOML.

claims.claim: base 프로필이 공통 설정을 담는 기본 프로필로 사용됨
claims.evidence: profiles.toml correctly places repository, password-file, and retention under ["base"], while documents and photos only specify their unique backup sources

claims.claim: documents와 photos 프로필이 base로부터 설정을 상속받음
photos 프로필에만 *.tmp 제외 규칙이 적용됨 (documents에는 없음)
claims.evidence: exclude = ["*.tmp"] appears only in [photos.backup], not in [documents.backup], matching the requirement

suggestions.reason: This assertion is checked only by human inspection. Consider adding a step that runs 'python3 -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open(...)rb)"' or a similar TOML parser to verify syntactic validity programmatically. The current check could pass even for subtly malformed TOML.

No assertion verifies that the documents profile does NOT have an exclude directive. The requirement states only photos should exclude *.tmp files, but an output that incorrectly added exclude to documents would still pass all current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the required fields well and all pass with clear evidence. Two gaps worth addressing: a machine-parseable TOML validity check, and a negative assertion confirming documents does not inherit the *.tmp exclusion.
